content.full_text:
NAPA COUNTY                                    OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL

                                                        1195 THIRD STREET, ROOM 301 • NAPA, CALIFORNIA 94559-3001
                                                        AREA CODE 707/253-4521  FAX 707/253-4176

ROBERT WESTMEYER, County Counsel

                                                        December 13, 1991
MARGARET L. WOODBURY, Chief Deputy
JOSEPH C. FOLKARD, Deputy
DAVID L. ZALTSMAN, Deputy                              Our File No. 1410.500
KEVIN C. STOBAUGH, Deputy
JAYNE F. HERMAN, Deputy




Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814
Attn: Legal Division

Ladies and Gentlemen:

Tony Holzhauer, a commissioner on the Napa County Conservation, Development and
Planning Commission (the "Commission"), hereby requests a written opinion from
your office as to whether a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act
exists based upon the following facts:

Commissioner Holzhauer is currently employed as a real estate agent for Up
Valley Associates, a real estate firm located at 1126 Adams Street, St. Helena,
CA 94574 ("Up Valley").  As a commissioned agent for Up Valley, Commissioner
Holzhauer assists the firm in marketing its listed properties.  Commissioner
Holzhauer currently has several listings in the unincorporated areas of Napa
County, but has earned no commissions in the past twelve months from the sale of
properties located in unincorporated Napa County.  He may earn such commissions
in the future should a favorable opportunity present itself.

The Housing Element of the Napa County General Plan states that the provision of
affordable housing in the County is to be encouraged in connection with the
development of residential projects.  The Commission is currently scheduled to
consider whether to recommend to the Napa County Board of Supervisors (the
"Board") that an inclusionary housing ordinance be developed and adopted as part
of a comprehensive response to this aspect of the County's General Plan.  Such a
recommendation, if made, could include endorsement of a model ordinance
requiring some or all of the following:  that a certain percentage of all new
residential units constructed in a project be "affordable" as that term is
defined in the ordinance; that a fee be paid to an affordable housing fund in
lieu of the actual construction of affordable units; or that construction of a
certain number of affordable units take place off-site.  In addition, the
Commission is likely to recommend the creation of additional sources of funding
for affordable housing through means such as assessment of linkage fees from
developers of agricultural, commercial and industrial projects based upon
project-generated employment, imposition of a business license tax or increases
in documentary transfer or sales taxes.  Should Commissioner Holzhauer ever

December 13, 1991
Page 2

become involved in the marketing of a property located in unincorporated Napa
County, such property would be subject to any ordinance ultimately adopted by
the Board.

The State Department of Housing and Community Development has required that some
form of inclusionary program be referred to the Board by December 31, 1991.
Failure to do so may reduce or eliminate the ability of the County to qualify
for Community Development Block Grant funds. We would therefore very much
appreciate expedited handling of this request and the return of a written
opinion to this office by December 26, 1991, if possible, so that a special
meeting of the Commission may be held on December 30, 1991. If you have any
questions as you conduct your review, please do not hesitate to contact either
me (707/253-4521) or Commissioner Holzhauer (707/963-1222 or 707/963-9250).
Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

JAYNE F. HERMAN
Deputy County Counsel

cc: Tony Holzhauer
Paul Battisti

[2000/request.oph]

sections.question: Whether a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act exists based upon Commissioner Tony Holzhauer's employment as a real estate agent for Up Valley Associates while serving on the Napa County Conservation, Development and Planning Commission, given that the Commission is scheduled to consider an inclusionary housing ordinance that could affect properties he may market in the future.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Commissioner Holzhauer have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act by serving on a planning commission while employed as a real estate agent, when the commission is considering an inclusionary housing ordinance that could affect properties he markets?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No CONCLUSION or ANSWER section is present. Document contains detailed fact pattern and context but is seeking advice rather than providing it. Urgent deadline noted (December 26, 1991 response requested for December 30, 1991 meeting).

embedding.summary: Napa County Counsel requests a written opinion from the FPPC regarding whether a conflict of interest exists for Planning Commissioner Tony Holzhauer, who is employed as a real estate agent, in connection with the Commission's consideration of an inclusionary housing ordinance. The request seeks expedited handling with a response deadline of December 26, 1991.
